threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You say 'the tube' when it's clearly the Overground around here. Locals just hop on the 67 bus like it's second nature.
Heard a guy on the 133 talking about his late-night fish delivery job—said he’s seen more drunks than the local pub on Friday. Pure chaos out there.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I heard a midnight karaoke battle on Karl-Marx-Strasse, complete with off-key but spirited renditions of ‘Atemlos’. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a nightclub that doubles as a gallery, complete with daytime art events. Dance till dawn, then walk through eclectic exhibits.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they roll their eyes at tourists gawking at BART delays, while still planning their day around those 30-minute gaps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo, top hat and all, breakdancing next to a Muni stop. Only in SF do transit rides come with a side of spontaneity.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that the best gelato is in the small shops, not the big ones. The lines are shorter and the flavors are real.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still look for seats on the metro during rush hour, while the locals squeeze in like sardines without a second thought.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 22 bus is a reliable option. Locals know to skip the bus and just walk; saves time and sanity.
Last night on the 47, a lad was singing to the driver—got a chorus going with the regulars. Made the late ride feel more like a pub evening.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a local kid selling homemade lemonade next to a graffiti mural, and the old man buying a cup gave him a thumbs up. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can grab a döner kebab at 3 AM while a street artist plays violin in the middle of the Kreuzberg night. That's Berlin for you.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot the locals by how they nonchalantly avoid the BART delays while newcomers stand confused, clutching their phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy in a tuxedo hop on the 31 at Turk, he looked like he just came from a wedding. Only in SF do you see that kind of vibe on the Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real gems are the tiny plazas where locals sip café con leche, ignoring the hype.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still take photos of the pavement mosaics in Gràcia, thinking it’s some hidden gem. Locals just walk over them, used to the beauty.
